codeReview.summary: The commit correctly hardens VAL-DAEMON-003 by adding a true equal-timestamp fixture path and deterministic missionID-ascending tie-break assertions, while keeping mission ordering logic unchanged and covered by passing daemon tests.

issues.description: The inline comment states the lexical order as `mission-z < mission-a < mission-m`, which is incorrect and conflicts with the assertions below (`mission-a < mission-m < mission-z`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker procedure/path guidance appears ambiguous for validation-contract checks: the worker concluded the file was missing by searching repo-root paths, even though the mission-scoped validation contract exists and already uses BurnBar* filters.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows worker probing `/Users/albertonunez/Documents/Windsurf/BurnBar/validation-contract.md` and then `**/validation-contract.md` (no matches), then marking contract alignment undone. Mission file exists at `/Users/albertonunez/.factory/missions/d1d1287f-f2a9-4a34-8ba9-fce37df13493/validation-contract.md` with BurnBar filters (e.g., lines 11, 56, 66, 103, 160).

summary: Review PASS. Feature behavior is implemented and validated for true equal-timestamp tie-break coverage in daemon mission ordering. One minor test-comment inconsistency was found, and a skill/pathing shared-state observation was recorded.
